The director has to arrange the scenes with shooting days 4, 6, 8, 10, and 12 in a sequence such that no two consecutive scenes come from movies released in the same decade. The release years of the movies are 1994, 2004, 2007, 2012, and 2015 which correspond to the decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, and {2010-2019} respectively. To minimize the total shooting days while respecting the constraint the optimal sequence is 1994, 2004, 2012, 2007, 2015. The total shooting days are 4 + 6 + 10 + 8 + 12 = 40. The sequence alternates between decades {1990-1999}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019}, {2000-2009}, {2010-2019} ensuring no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The total number of shooting days is minimized to 40.  
******  
The director has a budget of ₹50 lakhs and costs per day for the scenes are ₹1 lakh, ₹1.5 lakhs, ₹2 lakhs, ₹2.5 lakhs, and ₹3 lakhs respectively. The objective is to maximize the number of completed scenes without exceeding the budget. The linear programming problem can be formulated as follows: Maximize x1 + x2 + x3 + x4 + x5 subject to 1x1 + 1.5x2 + 2x3 + 2.5x4 + 3x5 ≤ 50 and xi ≥ 0 for all i. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is 4 by selecting scenes with 4, 6, 8, and 10 days. The total cost is 4 + 6 + 8 + 10 = 28 which is within the budget.
